title: Smoky Crumbly Filipino Pork Skewers
description: A classic Filipino dish featuring smoky, crumbly pork skewers with capers, perfect for a crowd. This Whole30-friendly recipe is easy to make and packed with flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together coconut aminos, apple cider vinegar, capers, garlic, black pepper, smoked paprika, and salt.
Add the pork pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread 3-4 pieces of marinated pork onto each bamboo skewer.
Brush the grill or grill pan with a small amount of oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F.
Transfer the skewers to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
Steam the skewers over boiling water for 2-3 minutes to add moisture and crumbliness.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional capers and smoked paprika if desired.
